LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANT MEDICAL RESEARCH INSTITUTE'S DEMURRER TO THE FIRST AND SECOND CAUSES OF ACTION IN THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO ANSWER. PLAINTIFF HAS ALLEGED INJURY FROM ILLEGAL CONDUCT IN CALIFORNIA, JUSTIFYING STANDING. PLAINTIFF MAY BE ABLE TO CERTIFY A CLASS AND EVEN IF DEFENDANT'S PRE-EMPTION THEORY WERE CORRECT, NOT ALL ALLEGED CONDUCT WOULD BE PRE-EMPTED. THE PARTIES SHOULD CONSIDER APPLYING FOR DESIGNATION AS A COMPLEX CASE OR FOR SINGLE ASSIGNMENT. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR# 8189

Off Calendar Mar-16-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES: Patrick N. Keegan, Esq., for Plaintiff and moving party: Curt Lavere; Alan Lazarus, Esq., for Defendant and opposing party: Medical Research Institute

HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED as modified: Plaintiff's Motion To Compel Further Written Responses And Production Of Documents To Plaintiff's First Sets Of Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, And Request For Production Of Documents is granted.
As to Interrogatory Nos. 2, 3, 4, 8, 9, 10, and 38, the motion is granted. As to present employees, their contact information may be through counsel. As to former employees, Defendant shall give the employees' last known contact information.
The motion is granted as to Interrogatory Nos. 13 and 14.
The motion is granted as to Interrogatory No. 26, except as it seeks the source from which each supplier obtains the ingredients supplied.
The motion is granted as to Interrogatory No. 53, limited to NO2 product(s)AS TO SAFETY AND EFICACY.
The motion is granted as to the Request For Production Of Documents Nos. 76, 86, 87, 88, and 89.
The motion is granted as to Request For Production Of Documents No. 117, limited to NO2 product(s).

The court approves the proposed opt out letter. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED.
